source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure to Regularly Review Security Configuration

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure to Regularly Review Security Configuration
input_row.description: This helps by reviewing the database factors from database engine, review instance details, security networks, encryption settings, audit logging, and authentication. By updating or removing a few things from these lists it helps tighten security and ensures that the users do not have excessive permissions.
input_row.rationale: Impact: Updating the system and being updated with security configurations keeps everything secure and prevents it from an attack.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console • Sign into the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon RDS Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/rds/. 3. Select the RDS Instance • Choose the Amazon RDS instance you want to review the security configuration. • Click on the instance name to access its details page. 4. Review the Database Engine Documentation • Refer to the documentation provided by the database engine vendor (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server) to understand the security best practices and configuration options specific to the database engine you use on Amazon RDS. • Review the vendor's guidelines for securing the database engine and associated components.   5. Review the Instance Details • In the instance details page, review the configuration settings related to security. • Security group associations: Ensure the appropriate security groups are assigned to the RDS instance to control inbound and outbound traffic. • IAM database authentication: Verify if IAM database authentication is enabled for enhanced security. • Encryption at rest: Confirm if encryption at rest is enabled using either AWS- managed keys or customer-managed keys. • Encryption in transit: Check if SSL/TLS encryption is enabled for secure data transmission. Backup and retention: Review the automated backup settings and retention period to ensure data recovery capability. 6. Review Database User Privileges • Click Users in the Amazon RDS console menu. • Review the privileges assigned to database users. • Ensure that the least privileged access is implemented, granting only necessary privileges to each user or role. 7. Review Audit and Logging Configuration • In the Amazon RDS console, navigate to the Configuration or Monitoring & Logs section. • Review the settings related to database audit logging and logging. • Ensure appropriate logs are enabled and configured to capture necessary information for security analysis and monitoring. 8. Review Network Security • In the Amazon RDS console, navigate to the Connectivity & Security or Security section. • Review the network security settings, including the associated security groups and their rules. • Verify that only necessary ports are open, and access is restricted to trusted sources. 9. Review and Address Security Recommendations • Periodically review the security recommendations provided by AWS through the Amazon RDS console or the AWS Trusted Advisor service. • Address any security recommendations promptly to ensure a secure configuration.   10. Document and Update • Document the security configuration settings and any changes made during the review process. • Maintain an up-to-date inventory of the security controls and configurations implemented for your RDS instances.
input_row.impact: Updating the system and being updated with security configurations keeps everything secure and prevents it from an attack.
input_row.justification: While several aspects of RDS security configuration (encryption, security groups, audit logging, etc.) can be programmatically checked via AWS APIs, the control explicitly requires a holistic and periodic review of the entire security posture—including subjective evaluation of user privileges, appropriateness of configurations, and alignment with organizational policies and vendor documentation. These elements require human judgment and cannot be fully automated, as not all relevant data (such as in-database user privileges or nuanced policy reviews) are exposed via AWS APIs.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Amazon RDS Console. 2. Select each RDS instance to review. 3. For each instance: - Review security group associations and inbound/outbound rules for least privilege. - Check encryption settings (at rest and in transit) via the console. - Review audit logging and Enhanced Monitoring configurations. - Examine database user privileges and roles (requires connecting to the database engine and reviewing user grants/roles, which cannot be done via AWS APIs for all engines). - Confirm authentication methods (IAM, password policies, etc.). - Compare all settings against current CIS, AWS, and organizational security best practices. 4. Document findings, note any deviations, and update configurations or remove unnecessary permissions as needed. 5. Schedule and record periodic reviews to ensure ongoing compliance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control requires a comprehensive review of numerous aspects of database configuration and security practices that involve subjective evaluations and documentation that cannot be fully verified through automated means. While certain elements can be programmatically checked (like encryption settings and security groups), the control explicitly calls for a holistic operational review which includes considerations such as user privileges, adherence to best practices, and compliance with organizational security policies. Thus, a manual audit approach is deemed necessary.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Sign into the AWS Management Console**
   - Navigate to: [AWS Management Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/)
   - **What to verify:** Ensure you access with appropriate credentials for security assessments.

2. **Open the Amazon RDS Console**
   - Direct access: [Amazon RDS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/rds/)
   - **What to verify:** Access the console for database instances review.

3. **Select the RDS Instance**
   - In the RDS console, choose the instance you want to review.
   - **What to verify:** Confirm the selected RDS instance is accurate for your review.

4. **Review the Database Engine Documentation**
   - **What to verify:** Refer to the specific documentation for your database engine (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL) for security best practices. 
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Follow guidelines provided by the database vendor for securing the database engine.

5. **Review the Instance Details**
   - **What to verify:** Verify settings for security groups, IAM authentication, encryption (at rest and in transit), and backup configurations.
   - **Expected secure configurations:**
     - Appropriate security groups are assigned.
     - IAM database authentication is enabled.
     - Encryption at rest is enabled.
     - SSL/TLS encryption is active for in-transit data.
     - Backup settings should align with organizational recovery policies.

6. **Review Database User Privileges**
   - Navigate to the Users section in the RDS menu.
   - **What to verify:** Analyze user privileges assigned for the principle of least privilege.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Every user/role should have the minimum required access permissions.

7. **Review Audit and Logging Configuration**
   - Access the configuration or monitoring section in RDS.
   - **What to verify:** Ensure enhancement in audit logging features.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Audit logging is enabled for capturing crucial security events.

8. **Review Network Security**
   - Check under Connectivity & Security settings.
   - **What to verify:** Confirm the appropriateness of security group rules.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Only necessary ports should be open and access limited to trusted IPs.

9. **Review and Address Security Recommendations**
   - Use AWS resource pages or Trusted Advisor to identify any recommended practices.
   - **What to verify:** Ensure prompt action is taken for any recommended changes.
   - **Expected secure configuration:** Recommendations addressed according to priority.

10. **Document and Update**
    - Keep a thorough record of configurations and any changes made.
    - **What to verify:** Documentation should reflect current security posture and compliance standards.
    - **Expected secure configuration:** An up-to-date log of security configurations should be maintained.

By diligently following these steps, you can effectively ensure the security configurations of your RDS instances are appropriately reviewed and updated.
